title: Fix run_once max_workers=0 crash in new slot-fill loop
description:
The new continuous slot-fill loop in src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py passes max_workers directly to ThreadPoolExecutor, which raises ValueError when max_workers=0. In the old implementation, max_workers=0 was a valid test idiom meaning 'run reevaluation but dispatch nothing' — it worked because selected was always empty so ThreadPoolExecutor was never reached.

Fix: add a guard at the top of the slot-fill block. When max_workers == 0, skip the ThreadPoolExecutor entirely and return result immediately after _reevaluate_blocked() completes. The reevaluation (expire_stale_leases, _reevaluate_blocked) must still run — only the dispatch loop is skipped.

Concrete fix in src/agent_takt/scheduler/core.py, in run_once(), after _reevaluate_blocked():

    if max_workers == 0:
        return result

This preserves the existing test semantics and restores the 20 failing tests in tests/test_scheduler_followups.py.
